old_claim.note: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim_absent: No dependency edge is retained because recombinant DNA technology was demonstrated before PCR and uses restriction enzymes, ligases, plasmids, and host cells rather than PCR as a necessary component.

source_shape.source_locator: Birth of Biotech, Recombinant DNA in the Lab; Cohen-Boyer experiments between 1972 and 1974.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the Cohen-Boyer recombinant DNA work as 1972-1974 experiments built from plasmids and restriction endonucleases.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the historical method predates PCR and names different enabling components, PCR cannot be a required prerequisite for the broad recombinant DNA node.

ontology_before: The 1973 recombinant DNA node depended on the 1985 PCR node, reversing the historical order.
ontology_after: The recombinant DNA node keeps earlier molecular biology and DNA-structure context, while PCR is represented as a later amplification technology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: recombinant DNA remains connected to molecular biology and DNA structure. Changed: the later PCR dependency is removed from the 1973 technology.
would_reject_if: A source showed the graph node intentionally meant only PCR-based recombinant DNA workflows rather than broad recombinant DNA technology.
A source showed PCR was used in the original 1972-1974 recombinant DNA experiments.
The graph split recombinant DNA into a later PCR-amplified cloning node where a PCR edge would be scoped correctly.
short_reason: PCR cannot be required for recombinant DNA technology because recombinant DNA was demonstrated before PCR existed.
